title: Smoky Savory Greek Lamb Baklava
description: A twist on traditional baklava, this dish features smoky lamb and savory spices, all wrapped in flaky phyllo dough, making it a unique and indulgent Greek treat that is diabetic-friendly due to its moderate sugar content and high protein and fiber content from the lamb and phyllo dough.

ingredients.name: lamb shoulder
ingredients.notes: rested meat, cut into small pieces

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and cook the lamb until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the smoked paprika, feta cheese, kalamata olives, and parsley to the skillet and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Layer the phyllo dough in an earthenware bowl, brushing each layer with lemon juice.
Add the lamb mixture to the phyllo dough and fold the dough over the filling to form a baklava shape.
Brush the top of the baklava with olive oil and bake for 35 minutes, or until golden brown.
Remove the baklava from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
Serve the baklava warm, garnished with additional parsley and lemon juice if desired.
